title: Abu Dhabi to ramp up crude storage, India’s reserve may go up 70%
content: India's crude oil security is set for a significant boost as the UAE's ADNOC agrees to increase crude storage to 30 million barrels. This move will add over four million tonnes to India's existing strategic reserves, enhancing energy supply stability amidst regional conflicts. Agreements also explore LNG and LPG storage, strengthening India-UAE energy ties.

title: Ex-Google CEO Eric Schmidt has message for engineers who are still coding the old way
content: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt declared the era of manual code writing is over, citing AI's explosive power. He believes programmers still using traditional methods are behind, as AI enables individuals to build powerful applications previously requiring large teams. This shift is causing a sell-off in software stocks, with companies needing to adapt quickly to survive.

title: Born in the USA? MAGA hawks go after desi doc's 'delivery' service
content: A US congressional inquiry has launched a spotlight on maternity businesses, including a clinic operated by an Indian-origin physician, amid accusations of promoting 'birth tourism.' Legislators assert that the practice takes advantage of immigration laws, enabling international families to secure citizenship for their infants in the United States.

title: Meta's highest-paid employee is not happy with 'just money motivated' tag for his team
content: Meta SuperIntelligence Lab chief Alexandr Wang is pushing back on the idea that his team joined for the money. On the Core Memory podcast, Wang said the real draw was compute access, talent density, and research freedom—not the $100 million packages Meta reportedly offered. He also addressed the viral soup story involving Mark Zuckerberg and an OpenAI recruit.

title: PIO Sikh man murdered British university student with 21-inch knife, UK court told
content: A British Indian Sikh man, Vickrum Digwa, is on trial for the murder of Henry Nowak, a British white student, with a 21-inch knife. Digwa allegedly stabbed Nowak four times after an altercation where Nowak reportedly knocked off his turban. Digwa's mother is accused of assisting by removing the weapon.

title: BTS breaks silence on historic FIFA stage with message of hope and unity
content: BTS will make history as co-headliners of the first-ever halftime show at the 2026 FIFA World Cup final on July 19 in New York New Jersey Stadium. Sharing the stage with Madonna and Shakira, the group said the performance is a chance to spread “hope and harmony” worldwide. BTS also highlighted their desire to support children’s education through the FIFA Global Citizen campaign.

title: Jannik Sinner and Laila Hasanovic’s 2026 net worth and earnings revealed
content: Jannik Sinner and Laila Hasanovic’s combined net worth in 2026 is estimated at around $35 million to $40 million. Most of that wealth comes from Sinner’s tennis prize money, ATP success, and endorsement deals with global brands. Hasanovic contributes through modeling, fashion partnerships, and social media promotions. Sinner’s growing success in international tennis continues driving the couple’s financial growth.

title: Canadiens’ Arber Xhekaj aids rescue during Montreal canal emergency
content: Arber Xhekaj reportedly played a big role in a scary situation near Montreal’s Lachine Canal as the Montreal Canadiens’ playoff series vs. the Buffalo Sabres rolled on. The Canadiens defenseman was able to spot someone in trouble in the canal and immediately called 911, reports said. The person survived, and Xhekaj’s cool demeanor off the ice gained a lot of attention in the process of continuous NHL updates and playoff talk.

title: Taylor Swift makes history ahead of rumored summer wedding to Travis Kelce
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce seem fully focused on their high profile wedding as the two recently turned heads in London as they stepped out for their date nights before flying to Greece to attend a wedding together, sparking shockwaves across the league. While the billionaire pop star is now back in the country as she was recently spotted grabbing dinner with her parents and childhood best friend, Ashley Avignone, it seems she has marked a new milestone in her career.

title: To ensure unity, Congress nudges Chennithala to join Kerala cabinet
content: Congress leaders are intensifying discussions on cabinet formation ahead of the new UDF government's swearing-in. Chief minister-designate V D Satheesan met with Ramesh Chennithala and K C Venugopal to finalize ministerial berths and portfolio allocations, with efforts focused on ensuring unity within the party.

title: 'No order without hearing us': Hindu party files caveat in SC after HC Bhojshala verdict
content: Following the Madhya Pradesh High Court's ruling that the Bhojshala-Kamal Maula complex is a temple of Goddess Saraswati, caveat pleas were filed in the Supreme Court. These precautionary legal notices were lodged by Hindu litigants anticipating a challenge to the verdict from the Muslim side.

title: PM Modi's UAE visit: How India will benefit from pact on strategic petro reserves, LPG
content: Experts have hailed the MOUs, calling them game-changing for India’s energy security in the long-term. The importance of having strategic petroleum reserves has been brought to light with stark clarity due to the US-Iran war.

title: 'Those who want to leave can go': Mamata amid internal dissent after Bengal poll debacle
content: Following a significant electoral defeat, West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has told unhappy party leaders they are free to leave, vowing to rebuild the Trinamool Congress. She urged loyalists to restore damaged party offices and reconnect with grassroots workers, asserting the party will not bow down despite the perceived "looting" of the people's mandate.

title: ‘Not an iota of truth’: PM Modi dismisses report of restriction on foreign travels via tax
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has vehemently denied reports suggesting the government was considering a cess or tax on foreign travel. He stated there was no truth to these claims and reiterated the commitment to improving 'Ease of Doing Business' and 'Ease of Living' for citizens, dismissing any notion of imposing such restrictions.

title: Samuel L. Jackson quote: Use your voice when you have the opportunity
content: Renowned actor Samuel L. Jackson, whose illustrious career spans several decades, champions the importance of advocacy through one’s voice. He passionately declares, 'If you have an opportunity to use your voice, you should use it,' emphasizing the vital role each individual plays in fostering societal progress.

title: Trump-Xi summit ends with twist: US staff dump Chinese-issued phones before boarding plane
content: US staff and press accompanying President Trump reportedly discarded materials from Chinese officials before departing Beijing. This occurred despite public displays of goodwill, as behind-the-scenes tensions over security and media access surfaced during the visit, echoing past diplomatic friction.

title: 'Did it as a favour to Pakistan': Trump on Iran ceasefire
content: President Trump stated the US agreed to a ceasefire with Iran as a favor to Pakistan, accepting the truce at Islamabad's request. This comes as Pakistan seeks to mediate the US-Iran crisis, despite reports suggesting it may have provided logistical support to Iran, raising questions about its neutrality.

title: Horoscope Today: Daily astrological predictions for May 16, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for May 16, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: ‘It motivates me’: Akash Singh reveals story behind viral note celebration - Watch
content: Akash Singh's electrifying IPL 2026 debut saw him claim three crucial wickets for Lucknow Super Giants against Chennai Super Kings.  His unique wicket celebration, involving a motivational note, highlighted his determined mindset.  Singh emphasised the importance of mental dominance and aggressive play, stating his goal is to 'dominate' in the IPL, a reward for his season-long persistence and hard work.

title: Fatah-3 vs BrahMos: Why Pakistan can't match India's missile might
content: Pakistan's Fatah-3 missile test sparks debate, potentially challenging India's BrahMos dominance in precision strikes. Analysts question if it's a Pakistani leap or Chinese hardware, highlighting China's growing military influence. Operation Sindoor showcased India's advanced capabilities, underscoring the importance of integrated systems beyond just missile technology.

title: End of an era: Robin Singh parts ways with Mumbai Indians after 16-year journey
content: Former India all-rounder Robin Singh has officially departed from the Mumbai Indians franchise after a 16-year association. Singh confirmed his exit from both the IPL team and MI Emirates, concluding a significant support-staff tenure. He was instrumental in the franchise's success, being part of all five IPL title wins and two Champions League T20 triumphs.

title: Sidney Crosby & Kathy Leutner’s $76M Net Worth Revealed in 2026 Report
content: Sidney Crosby and Kathy Leutner Net Worth 2026 has become a hot topic in all NHL updates and sports business discussions. The Pittsburgh Penguins captain is still raking in millions from contracts and endorsements, and Leutner is still enjoying a successful modeling career. Their long-term relationship, private lifestyle and continued financial success have kept them in the spotlight of hockey fans and celebrity sports fans alike.

title: Celina Jaitly pens emotional note for Preity Zinta amid legal battle
content: Celina Jaitly shared a heartfelt note for her close friend Preity Zinta after joining her in Dharamsala to cheer for Punjab Kings. The emotional post comes at a difficult time for Celina, days after Mumbai Police registered an FIR against her husband Peter Haag following her domestic violence allegations.

title: Not our war: Behind closed doors, UAE urged Gulf action against Iran, Saudis refused
content: The UAE sought a joint military response from Gulf nations against Iran after Tehran's attacks, but Saudi Arabia and Qatar refused, citing it as "not their war." This disagreement strained UAE ties with its neighbors and contributed to Abu Dhabi's growing frustration with regional partners.

title: Insensitivity and lack of concern, alleges Congress on Center’s silence over U.P. storm deaths
content: Congress leader Ajay Rai said that the Union Government failed to respond appropriately to the crisis, especially as Russian President expressed condolences to India after 111 deaths and widespread destruction in U.P.

title: One month of Samrat Choudhary Government in Bihar
content: Decisions like Satellite Townships, the Police Didi Scheme, a Three-Tier Public Support System, one model school per block, regulating private schools, prioritising Bihari contractors, and implementing a paperless e-registration system for land aim to reduce corruption, save time, and enhance transparency

title: Rajasthan seeks permanent membership in Bhakra Beas Management Board
content: The Minister said appropriate decisions regarding water storage up to the maximum reservoir level of dams under the BBMB should be taken expeditiously after the completion of necessary studies

title: Elgar case: NIA seeks cancellation of bail of activists Sudha Bharadwaj and Varavara Rao
content: The NIA said that Bharadwaj and Rao attended a meeting on the Mumbai Press Club terrace on January 19, where other co-accused in the Elgaar Parishad case were also present

title: Four-year-old boy rescued after falling into borewell in Punjab’s Hoshiarpur
content: According to officials, the borewell had recently been made operational, and the work to fill gravel and soil around it was still pending

title: Supreme Court closes plea of Goa Congress leader after his death from snakebite
content: Appearing for the deceased leader, advocate Abhishek Jebraj requested the Court to leave the larger question of law open while closing the proceedings

title: DMRC rolls out hydrogen buses in Central Vista
content: The initiative seeks to promote green hydrogen as a clean fuel alternative and strengthen environmentally friendly public transport in the national capital

title: Joyride horse dies of highly contagious zoonotic disease disease in Chennai; Tamil Nadu goes into alert mode
content: ICAR centre advises State to immediately isolate and monitor all equines housed with the infected horse, recommends testing in adjoining areas; govt. to regulate inter-State movement of horses

title: Monsoon to reach Kerala on May 26, says IMD
content: Last year, the monsoon set in over Kerala on May 24 — its earliest arrival since 2009; IMD’s operational forecasts of the date of monsoon onset over Kerala during the past 21 years (2005-2025) were proved to be correct except in 2015

title: Bay system to trigger wet spell, ease heat in Tamil Nadu for next few days
content: Many parts of the State may receive heavy to moderate rainfall on Saturday; weather system may influence wet conditions till May 19, says RMC chief

title: Growing ‘attacks’on the judiciary by ‘parasites’, CJI Surya Kant says
content: “There are parasites in society who attack the system... they don’t get any employment and don’t have any place in the profession. Some of them become media, some of them become social media, some of them become RTI activists, and they start attacking everyone,” the CJI observed

title: Film industry should avoid wasteful expenditure, but not at the cost of workers, says Kamal Haasan
content: Calling for an industry-wide conversation, he says together, they must evolve practical and sustainable operating practices for efficient film-making

title: Results show it is possible to raise quality without failing students: Sivankutty
content: Sivankutty congratulates students who have become eligible for higher studies after completing Class X

title: Seminar on influence of Western Ghats on farming and economy held
content: Principal Secretary of Department of Forest, Environment and Ecology Srinivasulu said that despite high ecological and scientific significance of the Western Ghats, commoners living around Western Ghats were yet to fully appreciate its value for the future of humanity.

title: Election Commission orders probe into voting by foreign nationals in Tamil Nadu Assembly elections
content: A report has been sought from the returning officers of at least two cities and five districts across the State on how the foreigners managed to vote despite the Special Intensive Revision of the electoral rolls

title: Finance Ministry increases export levy on petrol, reduces that of diesel and ATF
content: There has been no change in the existing excise duty rates for petrol and diesel for domestic consumption

title: Differences over Kerala Chief Minister selection will be resolved smoothly, says K.C. Venugopal
content: When asked whether Ramesh Chennithala will be part of the Cabinet,  Venugopal says the matter will be discussed by the party leadership

title: Muslim side to move SC against Bhojshala verdict as Hindu groups celebrate amid heavy security in Dhar
content: Dhar city Qazi Waqar Sadiq rejects the court’s recommendation that Muslim side can approach State government for alternate piece of land for mosque.

title: New I-T Act is taxpayer-centric, says Principal Chief Commissioner Vidisha Kalra
content: She encourages taxpayers, professionals, and trade bodies to actively utilise the I-T department’s digital initiatives and facilitation measures for seamless compliance and enhanced taxpayer service

title: State to tighten groundwater governance to prevent over exploitation of resource
content: Minister N. Uttam Kumar Reddy pushes for groundwater enforcement, recharge and regulatory reforms as the resource is over-exploited in 16 districts, including Hyderabad

title: Thiruvananthapuram registers 98% pass in SSLC exams
content: 32,218 students eligible for higher studies and 2,313 secure A+ grade in all subjects. City schools fare well.

title: Petrol and diesel prices hike updates: ‘Take votes first, then raise prices’, Congress attacks Modi
content: Amid pressure on fuel supplies, oil-marketing companies hiked petrol and diesel prices by ₹3 across all variants

title: Tamil Nadu govt. may gradually increase women’s rights grant
content: The amount may initially be increased from ₹1,000 to ₹1,500,  given the State’s financial situation, says a government representative

title: CBI conducts searches in Haryana bank scam case
content: “Searches were conducted at seven locations, including residential premises, business establishments and showrooms of jewellers, premises of suspected beneficiaries of the misappropriated government funds, and other private entities connected with the investigation,” CBI said

title: Pregnant woman burnt to death after moving car catches fire in Kozhikode
content: The incident took place around 9 p.m. when the woman was returning home along with her husband after a medical consultation

title: No question of putting such restrictions: PM Modi dismisses reports of govt mulling tax on foreign travel
content: The Prime Minister’s response follows a news report that suggested that the government was considering levying a tax or a cess or a surcharge on foreign travel but no final decision had been taken yet.

title: Bhojshala-Kamal Maula complex declared as temple by Madhya Pradesh High Court
content: The court said that the Muslim community may approach the State government for allotment of separate land in the district for construction of a mosque

title: Rijiju raises concern over alleged undeclared foreign trips of Rahul Gandhi
content: Kiren Rijiju asks the Congress leader to share details of travels with authorities concerned to avoid action for any violation of law

title: ‘Surgeries, finances, familial pressure’: NEET retest has aspirants fighting on many fronts
content: The retest date clashes with the UPSSSC Pharmacist (Bheshajik) Mains exam, scheduled under Preliminary Eligibility Test 2025, some aspirants pointed out.

title: Marginal increase in unemployment rate: govt. report
content: India’s overall unemployment rate for the month of April stood at 5.2%, a slight rise compared to 5.1% recorded last month

title: Shah calls for uniform global laws, coordination to end drug menace
content: Amit Shah said the world will have to adopt uniform laws for a common definition of prohibited substances, standardised punishments for drug trafficking, extradition of drug kingpins, and intelligence sharing

title: ED arrests two “masterminds” in SRMF properties case
content: G. Ram Chandra Mohan and Akash Malviya accused of using forged documents and posing as SRMF office-bearers to illegally sell prime properties; arrested after multiple raids in Delhi, Noida

title: State's duty is to ensure ‘living wages’ for workers, not label them as ‘terrorists’: Supreme Court
content: SC is hearing pleas against detentions under the NSA after Noida violence; instead of fulfilling its Constitutional mandate, State detained protestors as agent provocateurs, says counsel for detainees’ kin

title: Press Club of India urges Uttar Pradesh govt. to withdraw NSA against journalist Satyam Verma
content: The PCI urged the UP government to withdraw the move against Mr. Verma and free the senior journalist from detention, stating that the UP police invoked the NSA after nearly a month of his arrest.

title: Drones are no more eyes in the sky, they are claws in the sky, says IAF Chief
content: Air Chief Marshal A.P. Singh says unmanned aerial systems are no longer merely surveillance tools but have become offensive assets shaping the future battlefield

title: The rise of Satheesan: What it means for the Congress, Muslim League and Kerala politics
content: V.D. Satheesan is now the unquestionable leader of Kerala Muslims and Christians, around half of the State’s population; over the past five years, he has carefully crafted his political positions

title: ‘We condemn attacks on UAE’, says PM Modi, signs defence cooperation pact
content: India and UAE also ink agreements on building India’s strategic petroleum reserves and LNG supplies as Modi makes a brief stopover in Abu Dhabi

title: Will ensure safe return of people who fled their homes, says Suvendu
content: CM rejects allegations of post-poll violence; Opposition leader says several people have been forced to flee their homes as fear is prevailing in the State

content: External affairs minister S Jaishankar meets Iranian foreign minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi on the sidelines of the Brics foreign ministers’ meeting, in New Delhi on Friday

title: India will wipe out narcos, be drug-free by 2047: Shah
content: - Bharti Jain is senior editor with The Times of India, New Delhi. She has been writing on security matters since 1996. Having covered the Union home ministry, security agencies, Election Commission and the ‘prime’ political beat, the Congress, for The Economic Times all these years, she moved to TOI in August 2012. Her repertoire of news stories delves into the whole gamut of issues related to terrorism and internal strife, besides probing strategic affairs in India’s neighbourhood. Read More

title: Panda hugs denied: Trump returns home to Washington mauling after China flex
content: the visit underscored both the deep interdependence and growing mistrust between the world’s two largest powers . many american analysts concluded that Beijing emerged with the greater strategic victory: international optics of equality with Washington and evidence that the US is increasingly constrained in confronting Chinese power . some described the summit as America’s potential “Suez moment” .

title: 2 coaching centres fined for unfair trade practices & misleading ads
content: CCPA has imposed penalties of Rs 10 lakh and Rs 5 lakh on Motion Education . a majority of the featured students had enrolled in ‘I-Eklavya (Online)’ . the institute had claimed ‘1650+ CLCians in MBBS, IIT & others’ - a figure the institute described as cumulative selections .

title: Friendly nations can rely on safe commerce, says Iran FM Araghchi after meeting EAM Jaishankar
content: external affairs minister S Jaishankar and his Iranian counterpart discuss the westasia conflict and its implications, including the Strait of Hormuz situation . araghchi said he had a fruitful engagement with his counterpart, discussed regional developments and clarified that Iran will always carry out “historical duty as protector of security in Hormiz” he said india can play a greater role in this region to help diplomacy and promote peace and securitize .

title: US says El Nino just weeks away, earlier than expected
content: forecasts from international agencies suggest El Nino could be weeks away . it’s now likely that the weather condition will prevail during most of the four-month monsoon period as opposed to previous expectations that it’ll develop in the second half of the season .

title: Energy, defence ties get a boost as Modi meets UAE president
content: india and the UAE sought to boost their energy, defence shipping and technology ties, signing 6 MoUs and announcing investments worth $ 5 billion from the arab nation into India . modi reiterated India’s strong condemnation of the attacks on the UAE, without naming Iran .

title: NEET retest to be held June 21, digital format from next year
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More

title: SC quashes Sengar’s bail, asks HC for fresh hearing
content: former BJP MLA Kuldip Singh Sengar was convicted of raping an alleged minor in unnao, Uttar Pradesh . he was a public servant and was in a “dominant position” to be rightly convicted by the trial court for aggravated form of sexual assault under protection of children from sexual offences (Pocso) Act .

content: Aerospace giant Boeing on Friday confirmed that China had committed to purchasing 200 aircraft, as previously announced by US President Donald Trump during his visit to Beijing.

content: Castro is the brother of the late Fidel Castro, the revolutionary and longtime foil of the United States who led the island's communist government for decades.

title: PM Modi Arrives In Netherlands To Deepen Bilateral Ties
content: PM Modi is scheduled to hold bilateral talks with the Dutch leadership in The Hague, focusing on expanding cooperation in trade, investment, semiconductors, clean energy, water management, agriculture, defence, and emerging technologies.

content: Lebanon and Israel on Friday extended a ceasefire for 45 days, despite a new flareup in violence, the US State Department said after mediating talks.

content: Organised jointly by the Adani Foundation and the Akhand Jyoti Eye Hospital, the event is expected to mark a major expansion in affordable rural healthcare infrastructure.

content: Donald Trump's 2-Day China Visit Live Update: During the trip, US arms sales to Taiwan, China's controls on rare-earth exports, and the countries' raucous trade relationships are among the main issues expected to be taken up by Trump and Xi.

title: Israel Carries Out Strikes In Gaza, Targets Hamas Chief
content: Israel said on Friday that it carried out a strike in Gaza targeting Ezzedine Al-Haddad, who it described as chief of the armed wing of Palestinian movement Hamas.

content: The High Court cancelled the 2003 Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) order allowing Muslims to offer Friday prayers on Bhojshala premises.

content: While Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping kept up a cordial front on camera, relations between American and Chinese press and security teams were far more strained behind the scenes.

content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi reaffirms solidarity with the UAE, and pushes for an open Strait of Hormuz on the first leg of a five-nation tour.

content: The accused, who has been identified as Ayyappan, is an engineering graduate and had been struggling to get a job for several years.

title: "Will Impact Poor, Middle Class": Vijay Slams Centre Over Fuel Price Hike
content: Vijay took on the oil companies, underscoring that they do not reduce fuel prices when crude oil prices fall in the international market but continue to make huge profits.

title: Centre Imposes Export Duty On Petrol, Cuts Tax On Diesel, Aviation Fuel
content: The Finance Ministry, in a notification, said that the road and infrastructure cess will be nil on exports of petrol and diesel.

title: Trump, Xi And A Room Full Of Men. US-China Summit Photos Trigger Outrage
content: Gita Gopinath, an economics professor at Harvard University, took to X and described the all-male optics as, "A painting of the end of meritocracy: A meeting of the two largest economies and not one woman at the table."

content: Lalita Radha Rani Face, her husband Sundara Face and other family members performed the ceremony on the banks of the Falgu River, strictly adhering to all traditional rites and procedures to honour the memory of Lalita's brother.

title: Disputed Bhojshala site a temple, rules MP HC; muslims may be offered alternative plot of land
content: the court also struck down a 2003 archaeological survey of India (ASI) order that had restricted Hindu worship at the 11th century site to Tuesdays while permitting Muslim prayers at the monument on Fridays . the ruling validates Muslims’ fears, says jamiat The Union govt and the ASI have been directed to take over the administration and management of the site as a Bhojshala and Kamal Maula mosque complex .
